quoteLast: It rises on the 15th, at 6:08 a.
title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, December 1884, No. 3
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: On the meridian of Aeria it set at nine minutes to eight; at
quoteLast: , according to the calculations made by the Martian and verified by the Aerian astronomers, the herald of Fate would approach within the range of terrestrial vision.
title: Olga Romanoff
author: Griffith, George Chetwynd, 1857-1906; Jane, Fred T. (Frederick Thomas), 1865-1916 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: p.m.--no doubt the "official" time for starting--and with a shriek we pass from the brilliant station to the darkness of the river.
title:
Little Folks (December 1884)
A Magazine for the Young
author: Various

quoteLast: we alighted in a sandy plain absolutely without herbage, covered with loose stones, a quarter of a mile due north of the well, which is in the narrow gorge, forming the southern outlet of this small plain.
title:
Travels to Discover the Source of the Nile, Volume 4 (of 5)
In the years 1768, 1769, 1770, 1771, 1772 and 1773
author: Bruce, James, 1730-1794

quoteLast: and steered south for about three miles, until 9.25; then I had to change our course to south-south-east for about half a mile to where we tried to cross the river, but could not find a suitable place for doing so.
title: Journal of Landsborough's expedition from Carpentaria, in search of Burke & Wills
author: Landsborough, William, 1825-1886
